title: Lawful Basis for Processing
description: The privacy policy must specify the lawful basis for each type of personal data processing (e.g., consent, contract, legitimate interest, legal obligation).
coveredLooksLike: Clear statement of which legal basis applies to each processing activity, such as 'We process your payment data to fulfil our contract with you' or 'We rely on your consent to send marketing emails.'

title: Conditions for Consent
description: Where consent is the lawful basis, the policy must explain how consent is obtained, that it can be withdrawn at any time, and how to withdraw it.
coveredLooksLike: Description of how users give consent (e.g., opt-in checkbox), a statement that consent can be withdrawn at any time, and clear instructions on how to withdraw (e.g., 'Click unsubscribe or email privacy@company.com').

title: Special Categories of Data
description: If the organisation processes sensitive data (health, biometric, racial/ethnic origin, political opinions, religious beliefs, sexual orientation), the policy must disclose this and state the additional legal basis.
coveredLooksLike: Explicit disclosure of any sensitive data collected, the specific legal basis for processing it (e.g., explicit consent), and any additional safeguards applied.

title: Identity and Contact Details of Controller
description: The privacy policy must identify who the data controller is, including company name and contact details.
coveredLooksLike: Company legal name, registered address, and a contact email or phone number for data protection enquiries, e.g., 'Acme Ltd, 123 High Street, London. Contact: privacy@acme.com.'

title: Data Protection Officer Contact
description: If a Data Protection Officer (DPO) has been appointed, their contact details must be provided in the privacy policy.
coveredLooksLike: Name or title and contact details of the DPO, e.g., 'Our Data Protection Officer can be reached at dpo@company.com.'

title: Purposes of Processing
description: The privacy policy must clearly describe all purposes for which personal data is collected and processed.
coveredLooksLike: A list or table explaining each processing purpose, such as 'to process your orders,' 'to personalise your experience,' 'to send marketing communications,' 'to detect fraud.'

title: Legitimate Interest Details
description: Where legitimate interest is the lawful basis, the specific interest pursued must be described.
coveredLooksLike: Specific description of the legitimate interest, e.g., 'We have a legitimate interest in preventing fraud on our platform' or 'improving our services based on usage patterns.'

title: Recipients of Personal Data
description: The policy must identify the categories of recipients with whom personal data is shared (e.g., payment processors, cloud providers, analytics services).
coveredLooksLike: A list of third-party categories such as 'payment processors (Stripe),' 'cloud hosting (AWS),' 'analytics (Google Analytics),' 'customer support tools.'

title: International Data Transfers
description: If personal data is transferred outside the EEA, the policy must disclose the countries involved and the safeguards in place (e.g., adequacy decisions, Standard Contractual Clauses).
coveredLooksLike: Statement such as 'Your data may be transferred to the United States. We rely on Standard Contractual Clauses approved by the European Commission to protect your data.'

title: Data Retention Periods
description: The policy must state how long personal data is retained, or the criteria used to determine the retention period.
coveredLooksLike: Specific retention periods per data type, e.g., 'Account data: retained for the duration of your account plus 2 years. Transaction records: 7 years for tax purposes. Marketing preferences: until you unsubscribe.'

title: Right of Access
description: The policy must inform individuals of their right to request a copy of their personal data held by the organisation.
coveredLooksLike: Statement that users can request access to their data, how to make the request, expected response time (within 30 days), and that the first copy is provided free of charge.

title: Right to Rectification
description: The policy must inform individuals of their right to have inaccurate personal data corrected or incomplete data completed.
coveredLooksLike: Statement that users can request correction of inaccurate data, with instructions on how to submit rectification requests (e.g., through account settings or by contacting support).

title: Right to Erasure (Right to be Forgotten)
description: The policy must inform individuals of their right to request deletion of their personal data, and the circumstances under which this right applies.
coveredLooksLike: Explanation that users can request deletion of their data, circumstances where this applies (e.g., data no longer necessary, consent withdrawn), and any exceptions (e.g., legal retention obligations).

title: Right to Restriction of Processing
description: The policy must inform individuals of their right to request restriction of processing in certain circumstances.
coveredLooksLike: Statement that users can request processing be restricted while accuracy is contested, processing is unlawful, or the controller no longer needs the data but the user requires it for legal claims.

title: Right to Data Portability
description: The policy must inform individuals of their right to receive their personal data in a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format, and to transmit it to another controller.
coveredLooksLike: Statement that users can request their data in a portable format (e.g., CSV or JSON), and that they can request direct transfer to another service provider where technically feasible.

title: Right to Object
description: The policy must inform individuals of their right to object to processing based on legitimate interests or for direct marketing purposes.
coveredLooksLike: Clear statement that users can object to processing based on legitimate interests, and that they have an absolute right to opt out of direct marketing at any time.

title: Automated Decision-Making and Profiling
description: If the organisation uses automated decision-making or profiling that significantly affects individuals, the policy must disclose this, explain the logic involved, and the consequences.
coveredLooksLike: Disclosure of any automated decisions (e.g., credit scoring, content recommendations), explanation of the logic used, its significance and consequences, and the right to request human review.

title: Right to Lodge a Complaint
description: The policy must inform individuals of their right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority (data protection regulator).
coveredLooksLike: Name and contact details of the relevant supervisory authority, e.g., 'You have the right to complain to the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) at ico.org.uk if you are unhappy with how we handle your data.'

title: Data Protection by Design and Default
description: The policy should describe measures taken to implement data protection by design and by default, such as data minimisation and purpose limitation.
coveredLooksLike: Statement about collecting only necessary data, privacy-friendly default settings, pseudonymisation where possible, and regular privacy impact assessments.

title: Security of Processing
description: The policy must describe the technical and organisational security measures in place to protect personal data.
coveredLooksLike: Description of security measures such as encryption (at rest and in transit), access controls, regular security testing, staff training, and incident response procedures.

title: Data Breach Notification
description: The policy should explain what happens in the event of a personal data breach, including notification to authorities and affected individuals.
coveredLooksLike: Statement that the organisation will notify the supervisory authority within 72 hours of becoming aware of a breach, and will notify affected individuals without undue delay if the breach poses a high risk.

title: Children's Data and Parental Consent
description: If the service is offered to children, the policy must explain the age threshold for consent and how parental/guardian consent is obtained and verified.
coveredLooksLike: Statement of the minimum age for consent (e.g., 13 or 16 depending on member state), how the organisation verifies age, and the process for obtaining parental consent for younger users.

title: Clear and Plain Language
description: The privacy policy must be written in clear, plain language that is easy to understand, especially when addressed to children.
coveredLooksLike: Policy avoids legal jargon, uses short sentences, provides examples, and is structured with clear headings. If applicable, a simplified version is available for younger audiences.

title: Right to Withdraw Consent
description: The policy must explicitly state the right to withdraw consent at any time and explain that withdrawal does not affect the lawfulness of prior processing.
coveredLooksLike: Clear statement such as 'You can withdraw your consent at any time by contacting us or changing your preferences. This will not affect the lawfulness of processing carried out before withdrawal.'

title: Data Not Obtained from the Individual
description: If personal data is collected from sources other than the individual (e.g., third parties, public records), the policy must disclose the source and categories of data obtained.
coveredLooksLike: Disclosure such as 'We may receive your contact details from business partners' or 'We collect publicly available data from Companies House.' Categories of data and their sources must be listed.
